message:
feat: ranked endpoint selection, identity-hashed timer wheel, and intrusive scheduler hardening

Transport router load balancer replaces full sort with select_nth_unstable
for top-N endpoint selection, reducing O(n log n) to O(n) for
least-connections and weighted strategies when only a prefix is needed.

Timer wheel introduces a specialized TimerIdHasher that uses identity
hashing for u64 timer IDs, eliminating RandomState overhead on the
hot path since timer IDs are already well-distributed.

Additional improvements:
- Intrusive scheduler: expanded work-stealing fairness tests and
  per-core affinity assertions for pinned tasks
- HTTP/1.1 codec: tighter transfer-encoding validation with explicit
  rejection of unsupported codings before body parsing
- Service retry: budget-aware retry with per-attempt deadline shrinking
  and jittered backoff ceiling
- Combinator hedge: adaptive latency threshold recalibration on
  successful responses to prevent threshold drift under load shifts
- Runtime kernel: simplified epoch tick alignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
